next_step_text: JRA 6/23: Invited CPO and director of contracts to Horizon

recent_activities_14d.subject: Single-threaded: only 2 active buyer contacts (Michael Winters, Mary Stapleton), no C-level or executive sponsor mapped.
Mike to send a message to the stakeholder team using job posting catalyst to re-engage
Unmet commitments: RFI response (due early Feb, not sent), demo (requested late Jan/early Feb, not scheduled), Higher Ed
Close date expired: 2026-02-28 passed, no slip recorded, no buyer conversation to re-align timeline. Deal in limbo.

our_open_deliverables.text: Send AI-generated meeting summary and notes to Cornell team (intro call 6 Feb 2026).
Reach out to schedule budgetary discussion call (post-demo next step).
Get a definitive answer on whether Merlin intake guided buying works with requisitioning kept in Workday.
Provide the sample MSA and implementation pricing per the pricing-call requirements list.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Confirm attendees and finalize stakeholder list for broader Cornell business-stakeholder review (planned mid-March per demo wrap).
Confirm broader stakeholder attendees (Mary Stapleton, Collins Farr, Jay) and align internal procurement/IT partners before widening the group.
Return the executed order form / MSA redlines for legal review.

competitive_position.summary: Ranked by current threat: (1) Zip is the live primary threat, the buyer confirmed 'it is between us and Zip' (4/9 Mar 2026) and Henderson named Zip as the rival he was watching; strong on intake, per Zycus weaker on completeness of vision. (2) Jagger (Ithaca) and SAP Ariba (Weill Cornell Medicine) are the incumbents being displaced. (3) Coupa was evaluated but the CPO believes its value prop has faded (26 Feb 2026) — now low. (4) Fairmarkit and Globality are sourcing-only point tools, low integrated threat. Our wedge across all of them is one platform (intake + sourcing + spend analytics + Workday integration) with Merlin Intake in Teams/Slack as the hero.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: I've been between you and Zip, I've been waiting to see who kind of emerges at the top of the stack.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 6 Feb 2026 (Henderson) + SF Next_Step_History__c 4/9 Mar 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Out-position on completeness of vision and Workday-aware integration; make Merlin Intake in Teams/Slack plus autonomous negotiation the Phase 1 hero vs Zip's intake-only strength.

competitors.name: Fair Market
competitors.quote: we're talking to fair market... only because they can basically be totally not integrated
competitors.how_we_win: Position autonomous sourcing + spend analytics as integrated value vs a bolt-on accelerator that needs only a CSV.

competitors.name: Globality
competitors.quote: We're talking to a globality on the sourcing piece.
competitors.how_we_win: Same as Fairmarkit: differentiate on integrated suite vs standalone sourcing accelerator.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Competing against Zip and Coupa, even though the CPO mentioned his belief that Coupa's value prop has faded.
competitors.how_we_win: Reinforce the CPO's own view that Coupa's value prop has faded; keep focus on the Zip head-to-head.

competitors.name: Jagger (incumbent ePro, Ithaca)
competitors.quote: Jagger for ePro on campus... we've got close to 140 catalogs.
competitors.how_we_win: Frame Zycus as the unified replacement that rationalizes ~140 catalogs to 12-15 and removes the maintenance burden Cornell IT already flagged.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent ePro, Weill Cornell Medicine)
competitors.quote: We've got SAP Ariba for Weill Cornell Medicine.
competitors.how_we_win: Position single-platform consolidation across both entities (Ithaca and WCM) ahead of the 2028 Workday go-live.

competitors.name: Jagger
competitors.quote: Jaggers kind of the 800 pound gorilla. They've got the lion's share of it. We're coming from them in the marketplace.
competitors.source: Avoma Introduction call 6 Feb 2026 (Justin Ajmo / Henderson)
competitors.how_we_win: Incumbent ePro (Ithaca, ~140 catalogs) being rationalized to 12-15; frame Zycus as the unified successor that bridges to Workday.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: We've got SAP Ariba for Weill Cornell Medicine, Quali for the campus.
competitors.how_we_win: Incumbent at Weill Cornell Medicine; position single unified platform across Ithaca + WCM versus fragmented multi-ERP self-service.

competitors.name: Fairmarkit
competitors.quote: We're talking to fair market... only because they can basically be totally not integrated... feed them a CSV with our vendor master.
competitors.how_we_win: Concede the easy CSV sourcing-only path, then show end-to-end value (intake + sourcing + spend analytics) that a point tool cannot deliver.

champion_strength.source: SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c; Avoma discovery call 24 Feb 2026 (Henderson)
champion_strength.summary: Henderson is a strong, active procurement-level champion who has driven the whole evaluation and is named as the Champion in Salesforce, supported by Cooper Smith. The development gap is upward reach: as a week-3 hire he is unsure of his internal partners and has not yet connected Zycus to the economic buyer or IT leadership.
champion_strength.champion: Michael Henderson (CPO), with Cooper Smith (Director of Contract Management)

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged. Henderson (CPO, week-3 hire) was candid: 'It'd be silly of me or misleading to say that Cooper and I are the sole business makers, we're probably more gonna influence' and 'we haven't figured out yet how we make a decision' (24 Feb 2026). The only C-level signal is Zycus-side: the Zycus founder/CEO (a Cornell graduate) 'expressed a keen interest in being executive sponsor' (24 Feb). Cornell's budget owner (likely a CFO or Provost under broad austerity) is unnamed across all calls, tasks, contact roles, and the $450k 3-year deal. The 23 Jun Next Step invited the CPO and director of contracts to Horizon but no budget-authority conversation is logged. Mapping and meeting Cornell's economic buyer is the single highest-leverage move.

recommended_moves: Ask Henderson to introduce the deal team to Cornell's budget owner (CFO/Provost) for a sponsorship conversation.
Deliver the implementation pricing and written business case tied to Cornell's $60M savings target.
Book an IT-partner working session to close the Workday goods/services integration question and co-author the augment-not-compete narrative.
